summary: Split the large game-mode translation unit gm_1A36 into three configured objects: a shortened gm_1A36.c, new matching gm_1AED.c, and new nonmatching gm_1B03.c. The PR added matched implementations for the gm_1AED range, moved the later gm_1B03-range stubs and small matched helpers into their own file/header, updated splits/symbol metadata, and tightened several lbcardgame prototypes used by the new code. The decomp.dev bot reported +5528 matched code bytes and 46 new matches, with apparent broken matches mostly caused by functions moving from gm_1A36 to gm_1B03.
what_changed: Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so gm_1A36 ends at text 0x801AEE6C/data 0x803DD550/bss 0x80480DA8, inserted gm_1AED for 0x801AEE6C-0x801B0348, and inserted gm_1B03 for 0x801B0348-0x801BF948 with corresponding data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges.
Updated configure.py to build melee/gm/gm_1AED.c as Matching and melee/gm/gm_1B03.c as NonMatching between gm_1A36.c and gm_1BF9.c.

Removed about a thousand lines of gm_1AED/gm_1B03-range placeholders and helper bodies from gm_1A36.c, leaving gm_1A36 focused on the earlier range plus a matched gm_801AEDC8 input aggregation helper.
Added src/melee/gm/gm_1AED.c with matched implementations for gm_801AEE6C, gm_801AF0D4, gm_801AF250, gm_801AF568, gm_801B0264, and gm_801B0304.
Added src/melee/gm/gm_1AED.h defining the local state structs gm_80480DA8_0_t, gm_80480DA8_8_t, and gm_80480DA8_t and prototypes for the newly split functions.

Added src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c and gm_1B03.h containing the 0x801B0348-0x801BF948 range: many nonmatching placeholders plus moved small matched helper/accessor functions such as gm_801B0FF8, gm_801B6320, gm_801BEB68, gm_801BEBA8, and gm_801BF634-family accessors.
Deleted gm_1A36.static.h contents as the split moved static declarations into the owning new translation units.
Adjusted symbols.txt by splitting gm_803DD550 into gm_803DD550_jp and gm_803DD550_us, shrinking gm_804D6872 from size 0x6 to 0x2, and marking gm_804DAC10 as double instead of raw 4byte data.

Tightened lbcardgame.h prototypes used by the new code, including lb_8001B8C8(int) -> bool, lb_8001C404(int) -> bool, lb_8001C87C(void) -> u32, lb_8001C8BC(void) -> bool, lb_8001CBBC(void) -> int, and lb_8001CC4C(void) -> bool.
Added gm_1B03.h includes to gm_1BF9.c, it_2725.c, and un_2FC9.c to preserve access to symbols moved out of gm_1A36.h.
smart_moves: Split the monolithic gm_1A36 object at exact section boundaries before marking only the newly matched middle slice as Matching; this isolated match progress without forcing the much larger gm_1B03 tail to match.
Kept the later 0x801B0348-0x801BF948 range in a separate NonMatching gm_1B03 unit, which preserved the relocated stubs and helper functions while avoiding noise in gm_1A36.

Represented the language-dependent data at 0x803DD550 as two same-sized arrays, gm_803DD550_jp and gm_803DD550_us, matching the symbol split and enabling get_lang_val to select based on lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS().
Introduced small static inline helpers in gm_1AED.c for repeated matching-sensitive logic: gm_801AF0D4_inline, set_gm_804D6870_inline, gm_801AEDC8_flag_check, get_lang_val, and unk_inline.

Implemented gm_801AEDC8 in the remaining gm_1A36 range using local static accessors get_master_status and get_copy_status over HSD_PadMasterStatus/HSD_PadCopyStatus, producing a compact matched controller-trigger aggregation across four ports.
Moved prototypes to the headers that now own the symbols, then updated dependent source includes instead of leaving gm_1A36.h as a catch-all for functions no longer in gm_1A36.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large object, update all relevant section ranges in splits.txt together: text, data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2. This PR shows the split needs to preserve continuous boundaries across gm_1A36, gm_1AED, and gm_1B03.
Bot-reported broken matches can be relocation artifacts after an object split. Here, many functions became broken in gm_1A36 but reappeared as new matches in gm_1B03.
Data typing can be a prerequisite for clean matches: the PR corrected a data symbol into two language-specific tables, fixed a 2-byte sbss object size, and retyped a sdata2 value as double.

Prefer narrowing external library prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once call sites reveal real return types and arguments; this helps expression typing and control-flow matching in newly decompiled functions.
Keep split-unit state local when possible. gm_1AED.c owns static gm_804D6870, gm_804D6872, gm_80480DA8, and language tables rather than exposing them globally.
For matching state machines, local inline helpers can reduce repeated code and help reproduce compiler layout while keeping the top-level switch readable.
After moving symbols out of a header, search downstream users and add the new owning header explicitly, as done for gm_1BF9.c, it_2725.c, and un_2FC9.c.
naming_conventions: New split files follow address-range names: gm_1AED.c for the range beginning at 0x801AEE6C and gm_1B03.c for the range beginning at 0x801B0348.
Address-based symbols remain in use for unknown functions and data, e.g. gm_801AF568, gm_80480DA8, gm_804D6872.
Language-specific arrays were suffixed with _jp and _us: gm_803DD550_jp and gm_803DD550_us.
Small helper functions local to a translation unit used descriptive static names where behavior was clear, such as get_master_status, get_copy_status, get_lang_val, and set_gm_804D6870_inline.
Unknown struct fields retained unk offsets/names in newly introduced structs, e.g. gm_80480DA8_t.unk14 and gm_80480DA8_0_t.unk4, avoiding invented semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used object splitting in config and configure.py as the main matching tactic: mark gm_1AED Matching while keeping gm_1B03 NonMatching.
Used static inline helper extraction to reproduce repeated branch/control patterns in gm_1AED state-machine code.
Used ternary-like bit checks such as gm_801AEDC8() & 0x40001 and gm_801AEDC8() & 0x80002 in gm_801AF0D4 to preserve matching-sensitive boolean behavior.
Used explicit static arrays for JP/US language layout values and selected them through lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS() rather than folding the data into logic.
Used exact scalar types in prototypes and data declarations, including bool, u32, int, u16, and double metadata, to reduce casts and improve generated code shape.
Kept some M2C_FIELD-based helpers in gm_1B03 where struct knowledge was still incomplete, preserving matches without overcommitting to uncertain structure layouts.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code rising to 29.83% (+0.14%, +5528 bytes) and linked code to 22.10% (+0.14%, +5340 bytes).
The bot listed 46 new matches, led by gm_801AF568 (+3324 bytes), gm_801AF250 (+792), gm_801AEE6C (+616), gm_801AF0D4 (+380), gm_801AEDC8 (+164), gm_801B0264 (+160), and gm_801B0304 (+68).

The bot also listed broken matches and unmatched regressions in gm_1A36, but the diff and report indicate many of these are functions moved into the new gm_1B03 unit rather than code-quality regressions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match gm_1B03 after this split.
Search for future renames or struct refinement of gm_80480DA8_t.
Look for PRs that replace remaining M2C_FIELD uses in gm_1B03.c.
Check whether the duplicate #include <melee/gm/types.h> in gm_1B03.c was later cleaned up.
Search for follow-up symbol renames for gm_803DD550_jp and gm_803DD550_us.
